markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define MemoryFact records for extracted facts, subject/predicate/object, qualifiers, confidence, status, evidence, and conflict lineage. This MT belongs to MemoryGraphAndClaims. Implement the Handshake-native memory system translated from research into product contracts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Paper-derived memory architecture must be expressed as Handshake-native MemoryOntology, MemoryFact, MemoryPassage, ClaimLifecycle, ConflictResolution, and EvidenceSpan contracts.
Probationary claims cannot become stable retrieval facts without evidence, conflict checks, and EventLedger promotion.
scope.expected_behavior: MemoryFactSchema leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: MemoryFactSchema is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 91c204e6; MemoryGraphAndClaims group: 21 memory-graph tests pass on real PostgreSQL (ontology/facts/conflict/bridge/projection/api/fixtures); migrations 0240-0243 with down+rollback test; facts back knowledge_claims (reuse lifecycle+conflict authority), projections marked authority_class=projection.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-114 requires a Handshake-native MemoryFact schema (subject/predicate/object, qualifiers, confidence, status, evidence, conflict lineage) on PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, reusing the KnowledgeClaim lifecycle. The code delivers exactly this: migration 0241 creates knowledge_memory_facts as a 1:1 claim-backed S/P/O table with proper CHECKs, typed Rust CRUD, and a fact-level authority-label transition table, all proven by real managed-PostgreSQL integration tests.
findings: Schema is real PG DDL: migrations/0241_knowledge_memory_facts.sql defines knowledge_memory_facts with fact_id PK, claim_id NOT NULL UNIQUE REFERENCES knowledge_claims(claim_id) ON DELETE CASCADE (1:1 backing), subject_entity_id FK, predicate_key + predicate_term_id (ontology link), object_entity_id XOR object_literal enforced by chk_knowledge_memory_facts_object_shape, qualifiers JSONB, authority_label CHECK vocabulary, and a schema_registry row with authority_class='authority', mt_id='MT-114'.

Lifecycle/evidence/conflict are reused not re-implemented: the fact backs onto a knowledge_claims row that carries lifecycle_state (proposed/accepted/conflicted/retired), REQUIRED evidence spans (0137 trigger), transition guard (0200), and conflict machinery. This matches current Master Spec 2.3.13.11 (02-system-architecture.md:2704) KnowledgeClaim requirements.

Typed CRUD in src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/knowledge_memory.rs (create_memory_fact, get_memory_fact, get_memory_fact_by_claim, list_memory_facts, set_memory_fact_authority_label) with MemoryFact/NewMemoryFact/MemoryFactObject types; set_memory_fact_authority_label enforces a legal label-transition table (operator_approved cannot silently downgrade) in can_transition_to.

Real PostgreSQL proof: tests/knowledge_memory_facts_tests.rs runs 3 tokio integration tests against managed PG via knowledge_memory_fixtures.rs -> knowledge_pg_support (isolated schema, full db.run_migrations() chain incl. 0241). Tests prove relationship+attribute round-trip, claim-delete cascade (facts_tests.rs:71-84), object-shape CHECK rejection (facts_tests.rs:122-140), and label-transition enforcement (facts_tests.rs:145-218).

No deferred-live escape / placeholder: grep of the migration, storage module, and tests found no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'pending MT-'/Live*Unavailable on the fact path. The only 'SQLite' hit is a comment in knowledge_memory.rs:22 explicitly forbidding SQLite/in-memory/fixture fallback (fails closed with typed StorageError).

Non-blocking aside: MT scope.relevant_files names src/memory/ and src/ai_ready_data/, but the actual implementation lives under src/backend/handshake_core/src/knowledge_memory/ (fact.rs re-export) and src/storage/knowledge_memory.rs. This is a contract path-naming variance, not a code defect; all changes remain in allowed product paths under src/backend/handshake_core.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
